titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 627

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=808 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 14:52:15.627775+00 | translation moa=809 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 14:52:15.638731+00

p.cl:
Alexander Dei Gratia Rex Scottorum. Episcopis . Abbatibus . Comitibus . Baronibus . Iusticiariis . vicecomitibus . prepositis .
Ministris . et omnibus aliis probis hominibus Tocius terre sue Salutem .

Sciant presentes et futuri nos
diuine pietatis intuitu dedisse . concessisse . et hac carta nostra confirmasse deo et Beate Marie
et sancto Cuthberto. et Thome priori de Coldingham’ . et Monachis ibidem deo seruientibus . et in
perpetuum seruituris Illas viginti Marcas annuas quas nos et antecessores nostri percipere
consueuimus de Coldingham Syre nomine Waytingge . Tenendas . percipiendas . et habendas .
eisdem Monachis et successoribus suis In perpetuum In Liberam . puram . et perpetuam elemosinam .
adeo Libere . quiete . plenarie et honorifice . sicut idem Monachi aliquas alias elemosinas .
Liberius . quietius . plenius et honorificentius . tenent et possident .

Testibus . A. abbate de Melros .
W. de Bondinton Cancellario . W. filio Alani Senescallo . Iusticiario Scot' . P. filio Comitis Patricii .
Iohanne de Makeswel Camerario . Rogero Auenel . Dauid Marescallo . Aeymero de Makes
wel . Iohanne de Striuelin

apud Berewich' Sextodecimo die Maii . Anno Regni domini Regis
Octauo decimo .
Alexander, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to bishops, abbots, earls, barons, justiciars, sheriffs, provosts, officials, and other other worthy men of the whole of his land, sends greeting.

Let present and future know us, move by the love of God, to have given, granted, and made firm by this our charter, to God, Blessed Mary, and Saint Cuthbert, and to Thomas, prior of Coldingham, and the monks serving God in that place now and for ever in the future, the twenty annual merks which we and our ancestors we accustomed to receive from Coldinghamshire as Waiting. For the said monks and their successors to hold, receive, and have for ever, in free, pure, and everlasting alms, as freely, quitely, fully and honourably, as the same monks freely, quitely, fully and honourably hold and possess any other alms.

As witneeses: Adam, abbot of Melrose; William of Bondington, chancellor; Walter son of Alan, steward and justiciar of Scotia; Patrick son of Earl Patrick; John of Maxwell, chamberlain; Roger Avenel; David Marshall; Eymer of Maxwell; John of Stirling;
At Berwick on the sixteenth day of May, in the eighth year of the lord king's reign.
